name: BCAAs (Branched-Chain Amino Acids)
summary: Redundant if you eat enough total protein. BCAAs alone cannot maximally stimulate muscle protein synthesis because they lack the other essential amino acids needed to build muscle.
effect: No added hypertrophy benefit over adequate dietary protein; taking isolated BCAAs provides a submaximal, incomplete amino-acid stimulus.
dosing: Not recommended as a muscle-building supplement; spend the money on total protein instead.
safety: Safe, but essentially wasted money for those meeting protein needs.
